description: Monitoring scan of the seeded Olist DataSource — the 3 seeded anomalies are present in the DB. Captures the agent's typical detection output under measurement (PR D showed ~10-30% loose recall, so the golden may contain 0-3 hits + some false positives — that's the BASELINE behavior to regression-check against).

input.notes: Same MonitoringAgent.scan() invocation as fixture 01; the differentiator is the DB state. PR D's detection eval establishes that the agent's monitoring pass on this seeded data is noisy; the regression check is for STABILITY of that noise pattern, not for high recall.

golden_output.impact: Voucher payment value has cratered from an average of R$ 1,553/week in the baseline to R$ 320/week in the last 4 weeks — an ~80% collapse that signals voucher campaigns may have been deactivated or redemptions have structurally failed, eliminating this channel entirely.

Rio de Janeiro's weekly revenue fell from ~R$ 23,503 baseline average to ~R$ 15,553 recently — a 34% drop in the workspace's second-largest state, translating to roughly R$ 7,950 of lost revenue per week that will drag national totals if not reversed.

Debit card payment value dropped ~18% from a weekly baseline average of R$ 10,128 to R$ 8,343 in the last 4 weeks, suggesting customers are shifting away from debit — possibly due to friction at checkout or a preference shift toward boleto, which held flat.

scoring_config.notes: Monitoring output is an array. We allow [] through structural diff (PR D's data shows this can happen on the Olist fallback path). The similarity judge anchors on which anomaly metric × scope × direction tuples appear — drift in HOW MANY anomalies is captured as a real regression signal.
